channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Mace, activist dispute handshake that resulted in arrest
item.description: A disputed handshake is at the center of a Tuesday incident involving Rep. Nancy Mace (R-S.C.) that resulted in an Illinois man’s arrest, according to a police report and a witness who spoke to The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump invites China's Xi to his inauguration even as he threatens massive tariffs on Beijing
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has invited Chinese President Xi Jinping to attend his inauguration next month — extending a diplomatic olive branch even as Trump threatens to levy massive tariffs on Chinese goods.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Mysterious drone flights over New Jersey: Homeland Security shares new details
item.description: The large mysterious drones reported flying over parts of New Jersey in recent weeks appear to avoid detection by traditional methods such as helicopter and radio, according to a state lawmaker briefed Wednesday by the Department of Homeland Security.
item.dc:creator: Bruce Shipkowski, Fox 5

item.title: Southern California forecast of cool temps, calm winds to help firefighters battle Malibu blaze
item.description: MALIBU, Calif. (AP) — Cooler temperatures, calmer winds and a chance of rainfall in Southern California this week are forecast to help firefighters as they battle a wind-driven blaze in steep, rugged terrain that's driven thousands, including celebrities, from their homes in Malibu.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, JAE C. HONG and GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: House sends Biden bill adding judicial seats after veto threat
item.description: The House on Thursday approved a bill that would create dozens of additional judicial seats over the next several years, sending it to President Biden’s desk after the administration levied a veto threat.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WGN 9

item.title: Trump tells TIME he will pardon Jan. 6 rioters on day 1
item.description: The President-elect, who will serve his second term as the first American president to be convicted of a felony, was named TIME's 2024 Person of the Year.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-FBI informant accused of lying about Bidens agrees to plead guilty to federal charges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A former FBI informant accused of falsely claiming that President Joe Biden and his son Hunter accepted bribes has agreed to plead guilty to federal charges, according to court papers filed Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump is named Time's Person of the Year and rings the New York Stock Exchange's opening bell
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump rang the opening bell Thursday at the New York Stock Exchange after being recognized for the second time by Time magazine as its person of the year.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Proposed 'Trump' law to allow felons to run for office in Missouri
item.description: The "Donald J. Trump Election Qualification Act" would grant the possibility of those who have been guilty of a felony charge to run.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Rescue group saves 11-year-old girl floating alone in the Mediterranean for days after shipwreck
item.description: MILAN (AP) — An 11-year-old girl from Sierra Leone was found floating in the Mediterranean Sea off Italy’s southernmost island of Lampedusa, believed to be the only survivor of a shipwrecked migrant boat that had departed from the port of Sfax in Tunisia, a humanitarian group said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY and MARIA GRAZIA MURRU, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Juarez firefighters give kids reason to smile on Christmas
item.description: JUAREZ, Mexico (Border Report) – It’s an 85-year-old tradition that a handful of firefighters and a generous community refuse to let die. The annual ‘Santa Bombero’ toy giveaway for needy children will start at 8 a.m. on Dec.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Coffee prices hit record high after challenging growing season
item.description: Higher coffee prices could be brewing for consumers as the global coffee market smashed a 47-year record.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ohio lawmaker proposes bill to make ‘flag planting’ in Ohio State stadium a felony
item.description: Days after the postgame melee between the Michigan and Ohio State football teams, an Ohio state lawmaker has introduced a new bill that would make “flag planting” within Ohio Stadium a felony criminal offense.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, ABC 4

item.title: Proposed 'Trump' law seeks to allow felons to run for office in Missouri
item.description: A Missouri representative is proposing a repeal of a state law that currently prevents citizens with a felony history from running for a state office.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mueller, KRON4

item.title: Black denomination upholds stance against same-sex marriage. A gay pastor in its ranks seeks change
item.description: The African Methodist Episcopal Church, one of largest Black Protestant denominations in the world, has been wrestling with whether to change a policy that threatens to punish any of its clergy who recognize same-sex marriages.
item.dc:creator: Darren Sands, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump again named Time's Person of the Year
item.description: About six months ago, Donald Trump was sitting in a courtroom in lower Manhattan listening to a jury make him the first former president convicted of a crime.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Rural towns push to divorce from big cities, form new conservative state
item.description: New move to secede from blue parts of California is being pushed by group hoping to created a new state away from the Democratic-controlled Legislature.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ivy League murder suspect's alma mater UPenn silent on CEO assassination after professor's celebratory post
item.description: The University of Pennsylvania has refused to comment on alum Luigi Mangione's arrest or to condemn the killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American who says he crossed into Syria by foot is free after 7 months
item.description: An American who turned up in Syria on Thursday says he was detained after crossing into the country by foot on a Christian pilgrimage seven months ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Drones' reign of terror gripping Americans reaches fever pitch and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN drug experts say opium production has leveled off in Myanmar, but instability may trigger a rise
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — There has been a slight drop in the production of opium in Myanmar, the world’s biggest source of the illicit drug from which heroin is derived, experts from the United Nations said Thursday, while warning of strong prospects for future expansion of the deadly trade.
item.dc:creator: GRANT PECK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Bill Clinton says he’s open to talking to Biden about preemptive pardon for Hillary Clinton
item.description: Former President Bill Clinton said he is open to having a conversation with President Joe Biden about a potential preemptive pardon for his wife, former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: East Coast storm makes a mess at ski resorts as strong winds cause power outages
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — Colder weather began to return Thursday after a storm that swept up the East Coast delivered a blow to New England, packing powerful gusts that knocked out power along with a deluge of rain and warming temperatures that washed away snow and dampened ski resorts.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 men go free after their convictions were overturned in a case involving a discredited detective
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Kan. (AP) — Two men were freed Wednesday after their convictions were overturned in a 2009 double homicide whose investigation was overseen by a discredited white Kansas City, Kansas, police detective.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill Belichick, University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill finalize coaching deal: reports
item.description: Bill Belichick has reportedly been tapped as the next head coach for the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill.
item.dc:creator: Brayden Stamps, Fox 5

item.title: The burial site of the people Andrew Jackson enslaved was lost. The Hermitage says it is found
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — At least 26 enslaved people died on the Tennessee plantation of President Andrew Jackson between 1804 and the end of the Civil War in 1865. Where they were laid to rest is knowledge that had been lost to time.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: San Diego sheriff defies new policy to limit cooperation with immigration officials
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — The sheriff of the nation’s fifth-largest county on Tuesday defied a new policy to limit cooperation with federal immigration authorities, setting up a showdown over a new obstacle to President-elect Donald Trump’s mass deportation plans.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Customer recalls moment Luigi Mangione spotted in Pennsylvania McDonald's
item.description: New details are emerging about what happened at the Altoona, Pennsylvania McDonald's where police arrested Luigi Mangione, the suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare's CEO, on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WGN 9

item.title: Greene says she is safe after bomb threat
item.description: Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-Ga.) confirmed she is safe after reports swirled of an email sent to law enforcement containing a bomb threat targeting the lawmaker.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KRON4

item.title: FTC issuing refunds to Fortnite gamers after unwanted charges
item.description: According to the FTC, the company unlawfully charged players for unwanted purchases, let children rack up unauthorized charges without their parents’ permission, and blocked some users who disputed wrongful charges.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Stone, ABC 4

item.title: Lara Trump stepping down from RNC amid Senate speculation
item.description: Lara Trump is stepping down as co-chair of the Republican National Committee (RNC) as she considers replacing Florida Sen. Marco Rubio (R), whom her father-in-law, President-elect Donald Trump, picked to be secretary of State.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, ABC 4
